handoff.reason: Scoping complete: phased_implementation plan with 3 phases + 16 ACs + 5 decision records. Phase 1 (seam inventory) can begin immediately on CORE dispatch. Anchor case (chunk-converter.ts:60-63) requires ORCHESTRATOR ruling on Option A/B/C BEFORE its specific atomic-removal commit lands in Phase 2; the rest of Phase 2 can proceed for unambiguous seams. Frozen-fixture WO (CORE 282382a) is the live safety net.

execution_rules.phase_gates: Phase 1 (inventory + ruling-required surface) gates Phase 2 (per-seam atomic removals). Within Phase 2, per-seam removals are independent atomic commits; ruling-blocked seams skip until ruled. Phase 3 (closeout-ready handoff) gates cross-project /close-workorder.
execution_rules.atomic_commits: Phase 1: 1; Phase 2: 5-15 (one per seam); Phase 3: 1. Total 7-17 anticipated.
execution_rules.tsc_invariant: tsc --noEmit clean both configs (tsconfig.json + tsconfig.cli.json) after each commit
execution_rules.frozen_fixture_invariant: Per Phase 2 commit, vitest run against frozen-fixture must PASS (CI-blocking)
execution_rules.real_world_substrate_identity: AC-05a 2415===2415 + AC-05b 263===263 must PASS at close (verified via predecessor WO static probe verify-dual-ac.mjs); optionally per-commit

hard_constraints: Phase 7 archive MUST NOT be reopened

Predecessor archives untouched (rag-index-frozen-fixture-dual-ac-invariants, rag-index-single-analyzer-slice, cleanup-indexing-orchestrator-analyzerservice-param, path-normalization-fix, pipeline-indexing-rag)
Frozen-fixture file contents (src/integration/rag/__tests__/fixtures/dual-ac-frozen/*) are FROZEN — do NOT modify; HALT if a seam removal requires fixture change
Frozen-fixture CI-blocking invariant must PASS at every Phase 2 commit
AC-05a real-world 2415===2415 + AC-05b real-world 263===263 preserved at close (and ideally per-commit via static probe)
Phase 7 chokepoint INVARIANT block (lines 32-70 of __tests__/pipeline/indexing-gate-invariant.test.ts) untouched
Each seam removal must EITHER delete cleanly OR promote to explicit-failure semantics — silent fallback is the anti-pattern being eliminated
tsc --noEmit clean both configs after each commit
Cultural validator scope-mode + tracking validator scope-mode PASS clean (acknowledge pre-existing 14/15 stubs.json schema drift per STUB-044)
No new SkipReason enum value or IndexingResult schema field beyond what ORCHESTRATOR rules on the anchor case (DR-PHASE-7-B locked-additive)
Net negative LOC required (real cleanup, not zero-sum)

blockers.description: ORCHESTRATOR ruling required on chunk-converter.ts:60-63 anchor case (Option A: earn-keep via SkipReason; Option B: delete; Option C: keep but log) before its Phase 2 atomic-removal commit can land. Phase 1 inventory + other-seam Phase 2 commits can proceed without the ruling.
blockers.blocking_step: Phase 2 atomic-removal commit for chunk-converter.ts:60-63 specifically
blockers.non_blocking_for: Phase 1 inventory authoring, Phase 2 atomic removals of unambiguous seams elsewhere in the inventory

phase_transitions.summary: Phase 1 complete: inventory.md authored, 2 delete + 12 keep_with_justification seams classified. Anchor-case ruling: Option B locked per dispatch. No RULING_REQUIRED seams remain.

anchor_case_ruling.summary: Delete fileExists check at chunk-converter.ts:57-63; let readFile error path own missing-file behavior. Companion: delete dead fileExists() private method at lines 245-256.

communication_log.summary: WO-RAG-INDEX-SEAM-DELETION-001 authored under DISPATCH-047 (medium priority post-bulletproof-pivot-1 backlog, concept #3 of 6). Promotes STUB-RAG-INDEX-SEAM-DELETION-001 (CORE bbdeba5) into a phased_implementation plan with 3 phases + 16 ACs + 5 decision records (DR-SEAM-A..E). Phase 1 = seam inventory + anchor-case A/B/C ruling-required surface. Phase 2+ = per-seam atomic removals (5-15 commits anticipated). Phase 3 = closeout-ready. Frozen-fixture WO (CORE 282382a) is the live safety net; per-commit verification gates include both frozen-fixture invariant AND optional real-codebase static probe (verify-dual-ac.mjs). BLOCK-ANCHOR-RULING raised: ORCHESTRATOR rules on chunk-converter.ts:60-63 Option A/B/C before Phase 2 atomic removal of that specific seam can land; non-blocking for Phase 1 + other Phase 2 commits. Standing by for ORCHESTRATOR to dispatch CODEREF-CORE for Phase 1 execution.

Phase 1 complete. Seam inventory authored at coderef/workorder/rag-index-seam-deletion/inventory.md. Result: 2 seams flagged delete (anchor-case fileExists call site + dead fileExists() method), 12 seams earn keep_with_justification, 0 RULING_REQUIRED (anchor-case ruling Option B received from DISPATCH-2026-05-08-001). Phase 2 has exactly 1 atomic commit: delete chunk-converter.ts:57-63 + chunk-converter.ts:245-256. No other removals needed.

WO-RAG-INDEX-SEAM-DELETION-001 closeout-ready. Phase 1: inventory.md (3afd853). Phase 2: chunk-converter.ts fileExists seam deleted (ffe79e9, -28 LOC). Phase 3: communication.json + inventory.md final accounting (this commit). Gates: tsc clean, 87/87 rag-suite PASS, 4/4 pipeline invariant PASS, frozen-fixture invariant PASS, Phase 7 chokepoint block untouched, AC-01..AC-15 all satisfied (AC-06 real-world identity probe pending at close time). Requesting /close-workorder dispatch from ORCHESTRATOR.

communication_log.message: Workorder closed via /close-workorder. Audit PASS (cultural validator 11/11 scope-mode PASS, tracking validator scope-mode PASS). All 3 phases finalized. Commits 3afd853 + ffe79e9 + cfd485a pushed. Archiving to coderef/archived/rag-index-seam-deletion/.

decisions_log.rationale: DISPATCH-047 dispatch payload directed plan_type=phased_implementation (seam inventory before atomic removal). SKILLS confirmed phased: investigation is non-skippable because the seam list is unknown at scoping time. Anchor case judged ambiguous (three options with legitimate trade-offs); surfaced as scope_decision_required block per dispatch-047 directive ('If anchor case ambiguous, surface for ORCHESTRATOR ruling'). DR-SEAM-A..E recorded covering plan_type, anchor-case deferral, per-commit gates, fixture-frozen exemption, and keep_with_justification legitimacy.
